name: Mahan
story: Still Yet.

name: Melina Asadi
story: A 3-year-old girl from Kermanshah.
She was with her father when she was shot by security forces.
An innocent child lost her life.
The truth about her death was distorted by authorities.

name: Amirhossein Hatami
story: He was arrested after the protests and later executed.
The accusations were based on alleged “confessions,” the circumstances of which remain unclear.
He was one of the youngest victims of the current wave of executions.

name: Sepeshr Ebrahimi
story: A young man who was killed during the protests.
In a 12-minute video, his father is seen desperately searching for his body.
Hundreds of bodies can be seen lying on the ground, while families search among them for their loved ones.
His death represents the suffering of countless families.

name: Daiana Bahador
story: In Iran, women are not allowed to ride motorcycles.
Despite this, Diana stood up for her freedom and shared videos of herself riding on social media.
She consciously chose freedom over fear.
She was killed during the protests.
Her family was pressured and forced to publicly claim that she had nothing to do with the protests.

name: Danial Moradi
story: He was killed during the protests.
This image carries deep meaning, referencing Arash the Archer – a symbol of courage, sacrifice, and strength in Persian history.
His sister shared this video so he would be remembered exactly like this.
He represents many young people who stood up for freedom and lost their lives for it.

name: Yalda Mohammadi Khani
story: A 16-year-old girl from Karaj.
She attended the protests together with her father.
There, she was shot 8 times by security forces.
Despite her father’s attempts to protect her, she was fatally wounded.
She lost her life the same day.
